title: Seared Steak with Garlic Herb Butter & Crispy Potatoes
description: HelloFresh most-favorited recipe — perfectly seared sirloin steak finished with a rich garlic herb compound butter, served alongside crispy oven-roasted potatoes. A steakhouse dinner at home in 40 minutes.
instructions: Preheat oven to 450°F (230°C). Cut potatoes into 1-inch cubes. Toss with 2 tbsp olive oil, garlic powder, smoked paprika, ½ tsp salt, and ½ tsp pepper. Roast on a baking sheet for 25–30 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden and crispy.

Make garlic herb butter: mix softened butter with 2 minced garlic cloves, chopped rosemary leaves, thyme leaves, and chives. Season with a pinch of salt. Roll into a log in plastic wrap and refrigerate until ready to use.
Remove steaks from the fridge 20 minutes before cooking. Pat very dry. Season generously with remaining salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a cast-iron or heavy skillet over high heat until smoking. Add steaks; cook 3–4 minutes per side for medium-rare (130°F internal), or to your desired doneness.
In the last minute of cooking, add remaining garlic cloves and thyme sprigs to the pan. Tilt the pan and baste the steaks with the infused butter.
Transfer steaks to a cutting board. Top each immediately with a slice of garlic herb butter. Let rest 5 minutes.
Serve steaks whole or sliced, with crispy potatoes alongside. Spoon any melted herb butter from the cutting board over the top.
tips: Patting the steak completely dry is the single most important step for a good sear — moisture is the enemy of crust.
Let the steak rest after cooking — this allows the juices to redistribute and keeps the meat juicy.
A cast-iron skillet gives the best sear; if you don't have one, use the heaviest pan you own.
The garlic herb butter can be made up to a week ahead and kept in the fridge, or frozen for a month.
